title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 attended the third 'No Kings' rally in Boston Common, organized by groups including the ACLU of Massachusetts.
Speakers like Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren addressed political threats, military involvement, and immigration issues.
Over 3,300 similar protests were held nationwide, with Massachusetts hosting over 160, making it the second-highest state for rallies.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute set with 17 songs at the House of Blues in Boston on March 27, 2026.
The band, led by Michael Bishop as Blöthar the Berserker, used theatrical elements like fake blood and satire in their performance.
Highlights included covers like Chappell Roan's 'Pink Pony Club' and original songs such as 'Tormentor' and 'El Presidente.'

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum, among four Celtics players, are questionable for Sunday's game against the Charlotte Hornets.
Brown is dealing with left Achilles tendinitis and is considered day-to-day, according to head coach Joe Mazzulla.
Nikola Vucevic is out with a right ring finger fracture, while Tatum manages right Achilles repair issues.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands attended the 'No Kings' protest on Boston Common, joining over 160 rallies across Massachusetts on March 28, 2026.
Speakers included Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Ed Markey, and performances by Dropkick Murphys entertained an estimated 100,000 attendees.
Protesters expressed concerns about national direction and democracy, with veterans and churches actively participating in the rally.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest, part of over 160 events across Massachusetts.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the crowd at Boston's 'No Kings' rally.
The protest on March 28, 2026, included notable local figures like Rahsaan Hall of the Urban League of Eastern Massachusetts.

title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Thousands participate in 'No Kings' protests in Europe, opposing Donald Trump, with events in Madrid, Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam.
The 'No Kings' movement originated in the U.S. as a major opposition to Donald Trump's second term beginning January 2025.
'No Kings Day' rallies are expected to become the largest protest event in U.S. history with over 3,000 planned nationwide.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres on April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo.
Cast members include Alicia Carmody, a Providence restaurant owner, and Liz McGraw, a cannabis entrepreneur.
The show explores Rhode Island's social scene, family dynamics, and community connections.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff's Department to cut 50 jobs to save $4 million after $26.5 million budget reduction.
Sheriff Nick Cocchi cites statewide funding challenges; patrols reduced in Springfield, West Springfield, Holyoke, Palmer.
Cuts, not due to overspending, are linked to long-term structural funding issues since county government abolition in 1999.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council approved $2.65 million to replace firefighters' gear containing harmful PFAS chemicals.
Firefighters will receive two sets of PFAS-free gear after concerns about cancer risks from the existing equipment.
Massachusetts will ban firefighting gear with added PFAS from January following legislation signed by Gov. Healey.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley, executive producer of 'Henry David Thoreau' documentary, spoke at Concord Carlisle Regional High School about the film.
The documentary, airing on PBS March 30-31, features George Clooney and Jeff Goldblum among its cast.
Henley founded The Walden Woods Project in 1990 to preserve the land associated with Thoreau's legacy.
